full_prompt: Design a single historic-era dagger blade. The blade must be a single monolithic piece of medium-carbon tool steel (1084 with composition 0.84 C, 0.70 Mn, 0.20 Si by weight) heat-treated to a hardness of 58 +/- 2 HRC via quench-and-temper, with a total blade length of 250 mm, maximum blade width 40 mm at the ricasso, tip-to-point distance 200 mm from the ricasso, central fuller reducing wall thickness from 5 mm at the spine to 4 mm at the fuller floor, and a primary bevel grind at 20 degrees per side yielding a sharpened edge apex angle of 40 degrees. The blade must be heat-treated per an authentic historic-era procedure (normalize, austenitize at 815 C, water-quench, temper twice at 200 C), and the entry must include a metallurgy poster 24 in x 36 in at 150 ppi or higher. The blade must simultaneously pass: (R1) a lateral flex test in which the blade is clamped at the ricasso and the tip is deflected 30 mm laterally with no permanent set greater than 3 mm residual after release, which requires yield strength above 1400 MPa at 58 HRC, (R2) an edge chipping test of 10 consecutive chops into a 20 mm pine block with no visible chip greater than 0.5 mm on the edge, (R3) a tip point load of 150 N perpendicular to the tip with no permanent bending greater than 1 mm, (R4) a linear buckling eigenvalue under a 500 N compressive tip-thrust load must yield a first-mode load factor of at least 2.5, and (R5) the blade must have zero cracks visible under 10x magnification after heat treatment. Thermal analysis is required only for the heat-treatment process window, not as a pass/fail. Vibration, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver a STEP AP242 solid body of the blade plus a metallurgy poster at 24 x 36 in, 150 ppi, and a mechanical report listing lateral flex residual, edge chip extent, tip load residual, and first buckling load factor.
short_prompt:
Design a single historic-era dagger blade: a monolithic medium-carbon tool steel (1084) blade, 250 mm long, ~40 mm wide at the ricasso, with a central fuller and a 20-degree primary bevel per side, heat-treated to ~58 HRC via an authentic quench-and-temper procedure.

Ricasso is fully fixed in the grip/clamp. Load cases.
- LC1 lateral flex: clamp ricasso and deflect tip 30 mm laterally.
- LC2 edge chipping: 10 consecutive chops into a 20 mm pine block.
- LC3 tip point load: 150 N perpendicular to the tip.
- LC4 tip-thrust buckling: 500 N axial compression along the blade.

Also verify the blade is crack-free at 10x after heat treatment.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Austenitize/quench/temper schedule and 58 HRC target are read from 1084 CCT and tempering curves, not FEA.

prompt.title: Historic-era 1084 dagger blade, 58 HRC
prompt.brief: Design a 250 mm 1084 dagger heat-treated to 58 HRC with 20-deg primary bevel, central fuller, passing lateral flex, edge chip, tip load, and buckling tests.

material.name: 1084 carbon steel
material.heat_treatment: normalize, austenitize 815 C, water quench, temper 2x at 200 C

out_of_scope_but_real_world_needed: Grain size and retained austenite measurement
